writeup.raw:
SUBJECT: MetsMoneyline — March 12: New York Mets vs St. Louis Cardinals

# New York Mets vs St. Louis Cardinals
March 12, 2026 · Roger Dean Chevrolet Stadium · 01:05 PM ET

## 1. Short Recap
The Mets open their 2026 campaign on the road against the St. Louis Cardinals. New York looks to capitalize on their off-season acquisitions. The game marks the beginning of a spring series with both teams fresh and rested. No notable injuries or suspensions affect today's lineup.

## 2. Pitching Matchup
Sean Manaea starts for New York, coming off a challenging 2025 with an ERA of 5.64. He'll aim to improve from his last three starts, which saw a 5.68 ERA. Michael McGreevy counters for the Cardinals, posting a 4.42 ERA last season and a slightly better 4.28 ERA in his last three. Both pitchers face lineups they have limited experience against.

| Pitcher | Team | W–L | ERA | WHIP | IP note | K | BB | HR/9 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Sean Manaea | New York Mets | 2-4 | 5.64 | 1.22 | 2025 — 60.2 IP, 75 K, 12 BB | N/A | N/A | 1.93 |
| Michael McGreevy | St. Louis Cardinals | 8-4 | 4.42 | 1.25 | 2025 — 95.2 IP, 58 K, 20 BB | N/A | N/A | 1.13 |

| Pitcher | FIP | xERA | K% | BB% | K/BB | Hard-Hit% | Barrel% | Last 3 ERA |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Sean Manaea | 3.94 | 4.02 | 28.5% | 4.6% | 3.50 | 42.6% | 8.9% | 5.68 |
| Michael McGreevy | 4.27 | 4.67 | 14.5% | 5.0% | 1.38 | 40.1% | 8.8% | 4.28 |

## 3. Lineup Comparison
The Mets' lineup features new signings like Marcus Semien and Jorge Polanco. Bo Bichette and Francisco Alvarez provide additional power threats. The Cardinals' lineup lacks standout performers, with several players struggling in 2025. No notable absences or rest days are expected today.

| # | NYM Player | Pos | AVG | xBA | OPS | HR |  | OPP Player | Pos | AVG | xBA | OPS | HR |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Marcus Semien | 2B | .230 (2025) | N/A | .669 (2025) | 15 (2025) | | | Masyn Winn | SS | .253 (2025) | N/A | .673 (2025) | 9 (2025) |
| 2 | Jorge Polanco | 1B | .265 (2025) | N/A | .821 (2025) | 26 (2025) | | | Alec Burleson | 1B | .290 (2025) | N/A | .802 (2025) | 18 (2025) |
| 3 | Bo Bichette | 3B | .311 (2025) | N/A | .840 (2025) | 18 (2025) | | | Nelson Velázquez | RF | N/A | N/A | N/A | N/A |
| 4 | Luis Robert Jr. | CF | .223 (2025) | N/A | .661 (2025) | 14 (2025) | | | Nolan Gorman | 3B | .205 (2025) | N/A | .666 (2025) | 14 (2025) |
| 5 | Brett Baty | RF | .254 (2025) | N/A | .748 (2025) | 18 (2025) | | | Jordan Walker | DH | .215 (2025) | N/A | .584 (2025) | 6 (2025) |
| 6 | Francisco Alvarez | C | 0.238 (2025) | N/A | 0.769 (2025) | 19 (2025) | | | Yohel Pozo | C | .231 (2025) | N/A | .637 (2025) | 5 (2025) |
| 7 | Mike Tauchman | DH | .263 (2025) | N/A | .756 (2025) | 9 (2025) | | | Nathan Church | LF | .179 (2025) | N/A | .504 (2025) | 1 (2025) |
| 8 | Tyrone Taylor | LF | .223 (2025) | N/A | .598 (2025) | 2 (2025) | | | José Fermín | 2B | .283 (2025) | N/A | .794 (2025) | 1 (2025) |
| 9 | Ronny Mauricio | SS | .226 (2025) | N/A | .662 (2025) | 6 (2025) | | | Victor Scott II | CF | .216 (2025) | N/A | .601 (2025) | 5 (2025) |

## 4. Bullpen
Both teams start fresh with rested bullpens. The Mets' bullpen was solid in 2025 with a 3.71 ERA. St. Louis has less depth, reflected in their bullpen rating.

| Team | ERA | xFIP | Last 14d ERA | Last 3d IP | Rating |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| New York Mets | 3.71 | 3.88 | N/A | N/A | 70/100 |
| St. Louis Cardinals | N/A | N/A | N/A | N/A | 65/100 |

## 5. Key Edges
- Sean Manaea's 2025 FIP of 3.94 suggests potential improvement over his ERA.
- Mets' offense boasts a higher wRC+ (104) compared to the Cardinals (96).
- New York's bullpen depth is stronger, with a higher rating (70/100).
- Mets' lineup features several players with OPS over .750 in 2025.
- McGreevy's lower K% (14.5%) could lead to more balls in play for the Mets.

## 6. Today's Pick
New York has the edge in offensive depth and bullpen reliability. Manaea's underlying metrics hint at a bounce-back performance. The Mets' superior lineup and bullpen depth should lead them to victory.

**Today's Pick: New York Mets Moneyline**

PICK_SUMMARY: New York has the edge in offensive depth and bullpen reliability. Manaea's underlying metrics hint at a bounce-back performance. The Mets' superior lineup and bullpen depth should lead them to victory.  
OFFICIAL_PICK: Today's Pick: New York Mets Moneyline
